name: Ithaca 37 Shotgun
prompt:
Vietnam War era Ithaca 37 pump-action shotgun. Used by tunnel rats and point men in dense jungle.

Physical structure (~0.95m total length):
- Receiver: smooth steel receiver body, bottom-ejecting (distinctive feature)
- Barrel: 20-inch barrel, smooth bore, no rib
  - Front bead sight at muzzle end
- Pump/slide: tubular forend that slides back and forth for pump action
  - Grooved wooden pump grip
- Stock: full wooden buttstock, smooth walnut
  - Rubber buttpad
- Trigger guard: steel loop around trigger
- Magazine tube: tubular magazine under barrel, extends nearly to muzzle
- Action bars: two thin bars connecting pump to bolt (visible between barrel and mag tube)
- No pistol grip - traditional stock configuration

Colors: Deep blued steel (0x1a1a2a), walnut wood (0x5C3317), lighter wood pump (0x6B4226), dark metal (0x222228)
Orientation: Lying flat, barrel along +Z, right side up
Scale: Real-world meters (~0.95m total length)
